rows.text: The line comes from the "afterward" of this book. This book should be on all the Best-Of-Lists and if it's not, it's because it's too radical, too true. It rejects platitudes. In "The Blackbird Singing or Just After" the line "I'm so proud of you" gets shuffled and reshuffled until I'm wondering: Really? Are we proud? "Your love higher and higher the cut of the last scream is laughter." This book is poetry is music is dance is exhortation is spell on masculinity. So many lines bowled me over, such as, "There is no hero. The hero is the experience" from a poem that begins "Is it niggas is redundant or niggas is superfluous?" And: "I'm not coming to your rescue this season is for dancing to vintage Bjork." I'd quote the entire poem "Kipling-esque" because it's an indictment of Kipling plus very fun read. The poem is choreographed with anaphora. There's a poem called "Justin Bieber and the Lonely Niggas" that ends "he went about his business making hits." I'm not doing justice to the formal elements of the poem which are like prose poems with interruptions in them. And there are visuals with text on the left pages. Some of these pages have the most jolting lines. Why are you still here, reading this, put the book in your cart.
rows.title: "everything I've loved, I've been, or longed to be"

rows.text:
If you think Mondo films, serial killer trading cards, Hunter S. Thompson novels, and the Jim Rose Circus Sideshow are the spiffiest things since sliced bread, you'll probably like this book. But if you're looking for something with a little more depth, look elsewhere.

In a self-interview near the end of the book, Jim Goad asks: "What if the idea of equality is entirely fictional? What if the communists were just as bad as the Nazis...or worse? What if Christianity is really no worse than any other religion, and so we should all feel free to defame Judaism and Islam, too? Why weren't the so-called 'oppressed' peoples of the planet able to develop adequate organizational skills and self-defense technologies to fend off the evil imperialists? What if, despite the fact that we tell ourselves we're making progress, the world around us is actually getting worse...and for reasons which seem too unpleasant to consider?" Well, those are all good questions. So why doesn't he try to answer them, instead of wasting valuable page space with Charles Manson doodles and a history of Western masturbatory practices? That's what I mean by a lack of depth. There's too much time spent on fluff and not enough on serious issues.

I can understand using sex and violence as bait to get people interested in the magazine, but once they've been taken in, there isn't much substance here. All too often it's just shocking for the sake of being shocking, offensive for the sake of being offensive. After a while, having someone take a dump on your kitchen table ceases to be revolutionary and merely becomes unpleasant.

It's hard to figure out just who the target audience for this magazine was. The Goads claimed to hold hipster counterculture types in contempt, yet who but those types could this magazine have appealed to? I don't know how fresh this material was in the early 90's when it first came out, but looking back on it in 2008, these magazines read like a catalog of every countercultural cliche imaginable.

While the editors went after women and feminism with a vengeance, and touched on some racial issues, there were some politically-correct boundaries Jim Goad and his late Jewish wife Debbie did not cross. For example, the Goads were perfectly willing to repeat the popular mantras that Hitler was planning to take over the world and that the Axis powers were solely responsible for WWII, even though such claims were called into question decades ago by such reputable historians as A.J.P. Taylor (Pat Buchanan also has a new book on these topics coming out shortly; the publishers of Newsweek magazine apparently feel so threatened by Buchanan's forthcoming book that they ran a front-page story a week or two ago decrying such "revisionist history"). Surely a pair of editors as well-read as the Goads would have been aware of such alternative historical viewpoints regarding WWII.

It's also interesting to observe that while Jim didn't hesitate to use the "n-word" or to call the Chinese "ch*nks," nowhere did he refer to Jews as "k*kes." In one passage, he refers to "Jews and ch*nks," rather than to "k*kes and ch*nks." Why the inconsistency? I guess Jim must have had some awareness that it's not the n*ggers or the ch*nks that control the publishing industry, and that using the k-word could spell trouble for his career as an author. If you wanted to be charitable, you could say he was refraining from using this word out of deference to his wife, but his wife was also a female and that didn't prevent the magazine from being replete with nasty screeds about women (the worst of them being written by Debbie herself).

One gets the impression that this magazine was being pulled in two opposite directions. Jim Goad, the son of an Irish-Catholic plumber (he doesn't mention his mother's ethnicity), seems to feel genuine empathy for the heterosexual white male underdog, whereas Debbie Goad can scarcely hide her hatred of Middle America (check out, for example, the intro to the piece by Boyd Rice and the bio of porn star Colleen Applegate). You can't be for one of these things and against the other, it's an all-or-nothing deal. There's also far too much Freudian nonsense in here about guns being surrogate penises, etc.

Still, for $15 plus shipping, this is a thick, nicely bound and printed book that's hard to beat as toilet reading material. You might also want to get it just to have a copy of possibly the only book to have come out in the last 20 or 30 years that is completely free of typos and grammar/spelling mistakes.

One last thing: Answer Me! No. 3 contains a ridiculous article by Adam Parfrey, the homosexual-Jewish owner of the trendy countercultural Feral House publishing company, alleging that Steven Spielberg is a pedophile. He bases his assertions on loose word/image associations, far-fetched interpretations of movie scenes in which he finds (or rather, projects) sexual meanings, and dubious statements made by a NAMBLA representative (who went so far as to insinuate that Spielberg may be a member of NAMBLA). I'm not sure what the Feral House crowd has against Spielberg, but its disdain is conspicuous, what with Parfrey's Apocalypse Culture 2 anthology bearing an anti-Spielbergian rant by actor Crispin Glover.

I'm no fan of Spielberg, and for all I know he could be a pedophile, but if you're going to make such accusations in public, you had better provide stronger evidence than what was in this article. If anything, the article makes a stronger case for the author himself having pedophilic tendencies.
rows.title: Cheap thrills

rows.text:
As a heterosexual woman who adores men, but always gets to be "the friend" I had high hopes for this book. But the sea of hipster profanity left me wanting to run to a Bible Study. Honestly, how is saying Fuck-shit-cunt-pussy all the time "creative" let alone "funny?" The intro was the best part. I did get a few good laughs, such as how hard it is to get and maintain a tennis ball butt. The rest, not so much. Yes, it takes work to look like this, but Dolly Parton said that back in Steel Magnolias--its not news.

There was so much promise in exploring how a tom boy can grow up and still be a happily heterosexual woman, wife and mom. But it was all about "me" her potty mouth and, naturally, how fab NYC is. There's life outside of NYC people. Honestly.
rows.title: So much promise, so little delivered

rows.text:
FIRST OFF: THIS PRODUCT WASN'T EVEN AVAILABLE FOR PURCHASE UNTIL OCTOBER 5, 2010. WHY ARE THERE ALL THESE REVIEWS FROM AS FAR BACK AS 2007? AMAZON, YOU NEED TO SCREEN THESE REVIEWS MORE CAREFULLY.

Man. This is REALLY bad.

The first clue that something is amiss with Season 7 is the packaging. Sony, skanky as they are, did a beautiful job packaging Seasons 1-6. This season, the "honors" went to Shout! Factory. It's like going from the Hilton to the gutter. However, I had purchased "Designing Women" Seasons 1-3, all of which were done by Shout! Factory, and they did a great job, so, although disappointed by the packaging, I didn't think much of it.

When you finally get to the episode selection menu, you'll start to get a sinking feeling. How in the world do you screw up the episode selection page? Well, Shout! Factory has managed it. I have never seen anything so cheap and cheesy looking as part of a DVD set. But OK--who cares, right? I bought this for the series, not the intro stuff.

Well, they've saved the "best" for last. Wait till you get to the meat-and-potatoes of this thing. Shout! Factory has taken this classic series, originally recorded on tape, and TRANSFERRED IT TO FILM. That alone completely destroys the integrity of the original episodes--but don't worry: they didn't stop there. They "enhanced" it. Kind of like the way going to the bathroom "enhances" one's toilet. They have HD'ed or 3-D'ed this thing to death. The result: a comedy that would've scared Hitchcock: it looks like a cartoon that's been filmed in stop-action animation--like bad Claymation. Also, the actors' body parts frequently appear to be out of proportion with one another, and the actors themselves frequently appear out of proportion with each other. (You gotta see it to believe it.) It's the creepiest, most bizarre thing I have ever seen; I can only describe the final product as grotesque.

They also decided to edit out certain naughty words that would have certainly offended viewers' virgin ears. For example, "Goddamn" is replaced with "Rotten damn" in the "The Draft Dodger". I guess they were afraid they'd offend those folks who never objected to hearing "Coon", "Chink", "Jap", "Gook", "Mick", "Spic", "Fairy", "Fag", "Queer", "Hebe", and "Polock" for the previous 6 seasons. Thank God--I mean, goodness--Shout! Factory is there to protect us from this blasphemy through their censorship. But what about all the lip-readers? Oh my... er... goodness!

Yes, this is the season where the writing goes downhill. But the writing is nothing compared to carnage awaiting the viewer. These episodes were recently aired on TV Land (or was it Nick at Night?) and looked great! This is an abomination... and that's putting it mildly. How can a series look BETTER on cable than on firsthand DVD?

CONCLUSION: Unless you are a major die-hard fan, I strongly recommend that you sit back, enjoy Seasons 1-6, and mourn what could've been.
rows.title: DANGER, WILL ROBINSON, DANGER!!!

rows.text: I was extremely skeptical of this … I mean let’s face it, it’s just a giant container of the stickiest substance on earth lmfao… but when I tell you this stuff works… IT WORKS..!!! Here’s the story!! …… So it all started when A pair of pigeons laid two eggs on my balcony I could very well of just threw them off my balcony and got rid of the problem from the beginning but I was trying to be a decent human being and decided to let them stay and raise the little chicks… Big mistake I should’ve been like the rest of society and been a shitty human being a.k.a. throwing the eggs off the balcony… had I known what was to come… Approximately 21 days later the eggs hatched give or take a few days I did a bunch of research online it said from start to finish it should take no longer than three months total time from the time of conception for the eggs to hatch grow up and fly away and leave the nest… this is the prime reason I do not believe everything I read on the Internet… Five months later I had a family of pigeons living on my balcony they had taken over shit everywhere all over everything and refused to leave no matter what I did they were not afraid of me due to the fact that I gave them water and birdseed when they were first born keep in mind this was 3 to 4 months ago those little bastards not forget those 2 to 3 days of birdseed and water and they decided to hold onto Hope that I would bring it out to them again and they have held onto that hope for the last three months going on 4… I decided I needed to do something immediately but I did not want it to be something heinous and I did not want to hurt them even though I secretly didn’t want to strangle them at times they were loud dirty disgusting and disrespectful… not to mention I was on the second story The two chicks that the parents had refused to learn how to fly so they just walked around my porch shitting and cooing all day waiting for their parents to bring them food… Refusing to flying off and leave the nest, just wanting to get taken care of, get fed , and be in a free shelter that they did not have to build and eat food they did not have to work for!!! kind of like the millennials of today… I was not above throwing these little bastards off the balcony and giving them a flying lesson myself but as I said I am in an apartment building and I don’t think the neighbors would appreciate that it might be frowned upon… Plus I have a two year-old daughter who loves to look out the sliding glass door at the birds… I don’t think me throwing her pets off the balcony is exactly the type of childhood memory I want her to have lmfao .. eventually I ended up putting a towel over each one and carrying it downstairs and placing it on the ground… I thought this would get rid of my pigeon problem boy was I wrong lol the very next day I go to the balcony to clean it up from the months of nasty pigeon crap that had been festering all over it I wanted my porch back I wanted my sanity back… And lo and behold those two freaking flightless pigeons learned how to fly overnight apparently and were sitting on the porch once again… so I took them downstairs again… And again… And again… And again… This went on for the next week straight multiple times a day… so let’s fast forward about two weeks after I begin trying different methods to get rid of these birds as I said nothing worked the pigeon spray where it stinks to high heaven and is supposed to make them not want to be around it they did not care they walked directly on top of it hell they slept on it… The pigeon spikes they did not care they flew over them and would perch on anything else that the spikes were not on… just when I was looking up BB guns on Amazon I came across this pigeon glue and I thought what the hell it’s cheap I’ll try one last hurrah before I get rid of these damn birds forever and ever! I’m glad I read the reviews first or I would’ve for lack of a better phrase fu$&ed myself!! If you buy this product and I cannot stress this enough please please please please be sure to put down tape preferably weather resistant tape on the surface of anything that you were going to put this glue on because I don’t see how he would get it off of anything once it’s on there that is how messy this stuff is I read another persons review who said they put down painters tape and put this pigeon glue on it brilliant idea only thing was I could not have bright blue painters tape hanging off of my balcony so I got clear weather resistant scotch tape The thickest one I could find to take up the most space and use the least amount of tape I covered the entire top part of my balcony with it and every single surface I could find on my balcony which wasn’t much considering I had nothing out there on the porch anymore since I cleared it all out hoping that would get rid of the birds (another failed attempt by the way).. so once I was finished covering the balcony railings with the tape, a porch light cover with the tape, the little trashcan I had outside for my babies stinky diapers with the tape etc.. I then proceeded to cover every surface I could with this glue of the Gods!!  (BTW: live in Las Vegas it gets about 110 to 112° or hotter on a regular basis in the summer here so needless to say this stuff does not melt lol it also gets pretty cold here in the winter this stuff does not freeze either at least not that I’ve seen it’s extremely weather resistant just thought I’d throw that in there) so once I told the little squatters  one last Fu#k you and the finger before heading inside I thought to myself if this doesn’t work I’m moving… The very next day I was taking my daughter to her doctor appointment I looked up on my balcony and I saw the pigeons swarm my balcony as if to mock me.. I was pissed I thought the product had failed… I even saw one of them on top of the light which I also covered in the sticky stuff if you recall what I said earlier lol as I watch them I also noticed they were trying to twist their feet but it kept getting stuck… I noticed the reason they were flocking and swarming was because they were trying to help the ones that were stuck in the glue LMAO and we’re getting stuck themselves by the time I got back from the doctors there was not a pigeon left on my balcony they were all on the neighbors balcony LMAO and they were all touching their feet and walking funny I’m guessing because there feet were sticking as they were walking!! Lmfao ever since then I saw two maybe three more times a pigeon land on the balcony railing and immediately fly off this was on the second day but ever since that second day I have not seen one pigeon come near my balcony!!!! I see them on my neighbor‘s balcony all the time as a matter fact I believe they laid eggs over there and started another family LOL but as far as my balcony goes not a pigeon in sight lol!!! I recommend this product 100% I have told all my friends about it as pigeons are a big problem in Las Vegas!! So if you’re ever looking at this and wondering does it really work I vouch for it and I would buy it again and again and again!!
rows.title: SAVED MULTIPLE PIGEONS LIVES..

rows.text:
Ok, I know that GO FISH has some bad acting. I know that the lines are sometimes self conscious and try too hard. And I understand that some people find the actors to be unattractive. But here's the thing: any movie made by dykes, for dykes, is a valuable part of our community, even ones that make us want to cry (in the bad way). So it doesn't represent you and your friends. Ok, well, maybe you haven't noticed that dykes are an extremely varied group of people, so shut up and make your own movie about your friends. And then everyone can complain about how it isn't just like them.

Maybe I'm biased because GO FISH was the first lesbian made movie I ever saw in a movie theater. Maybe I'm biased because the fashion in the film, horrible as it is, is EXACTLY the kind of clothing that my friends and I wore in the 90's. But I kind of like this movie. LIke it or not, a lot of dykes are really mostly interested in sex and dating and finding someone to love. Is that so bad? In my experience, straight people aren't all that different. And film in the 90's was really into that "let's just show people how they really are" thing- remember Reality Bites? No one gets all mad if straight people in straight movies talk and think about sex all of the time- why should dykes be any different?

Some of the things I really admire about this movie: it deals honestly with the "when are you a dyke, when are you bisexual" question, it has more than one dyke character of color, it has a family situation where the parents aren't ok with their dyke daughter (that's called reality, not like Better than Chocolate), the women are all normal bodied, with stomach rolls and hairy legs, just like the women I know, it actually talks about safe sex (unheard of in dyke film- did you catch it, when Daria gives Ely a ziploc bag before her date? uh huh), and, for that matter, the slutty character is the one who practices safe sex, a really valuable message for dykes.

So this movie will always remain one of my favorites. To me, it absolutely marks a time and place in dyke history. And as much as I hate to say it, apparently the 90's are now a part of dyke history....
rows.title: An excellent document of it's time

rows.text:
Nothing good comes to mind when you hear the words "Straight to Video." The first image that comes to my mind are those cheap knock-off movies that used to fill the local Blockbuster's shelf in the '90s starring low-end TV actors or washed up stars desperate for title roles. So, when I saw that Mel Gibson's 2012 action film "Get the Gringo" was going straight to DVD/Blu-ray I immediately expected the worst... but, much to my surprise, when I finally sat down with the film I discovered a solidly entertaining action romp. I'd go far as to say that "Get the Gringo" is the most entertaining film from writer/star Mel Gibson since his *ahem* incident.

Get the Gringo is directed with a good amount of style by Adrian Grunberg (who previously worked with Gibson as the Assistant Director on Apocalypto) and stars Mel Gibson as "Driver," a wisecracking, inner-monologuing criminal who winds up in a Mexican prison following a botched job. The prison isn't anything like what we have here in the states, seeming more like a ghetto mixed with a flea market that a correctional facility (having not researched this topic, I'll take it on the filmmaker's word when they said they were inspired by a few actual prisons in Mexico) There, he In the prison he befriends a young boy who is kept as the local crime boss's replacement kidney (you see, he's already gone through several kidneys, including the boy's father). This is the set up for a madcap action thriller with Driver trying to get back his money and protect the boy and his mother from the crime boss.

As you probably could gather, Get the Gringo doesn't feature Braveheart quality writing, but, for what it is, "Get the Gringo" is a solid action-thriller that reminds us why we used to love seeing Mel Gibson on the movie screen. He plays Driver with that same cocksure charisma and energy that made him one of the greatest action stars of the '80s-'90s. Very few actors pull of this kind of hero as well as Gibson and it really i

Unfortunately for "Get the Gringo", Gibson let alcohol get the better of him prior to Get the Gringo's intended release, which resulted in another leaked tape and 20th Century Fox choosing to release the film straight to DVD/Blu-Ray. It's a shame, because "Get the Gringo" is a much better return to form for Gibson as an action star than 2010's Edge of Darkness.

If you're a fan of classic Gibson action roles (and can separate the actor's work from his tumultuous personal life) you'll probably have a lot of fun with Get the Gringo. It isn't a great action film, but it does have a remarkably entertaining performance from Gibson, and a handful of solid, stylish action sequences certain to entertain fans of the genre.
rows.title: A Solid Return to form for Gibson

rows.text: Styles P has shown the most consistency out of all The Lox and he keeps that moving with this release. The first single Blow Your Mind(feat Swizz Beatz) is an updated version of Good Times(I Get High) but a lot better. Alone In The Street and Shoot N***** are pretty good tracks too. All I Know Is Pain is fire. Styles P's rhymes well over a dark beat provided by the HUGELY underrated Alchemist. Akon makes an appearance on Got My Eyes On You-- a track where Styles P explains to those would-be gangstas that he's watching them. Styles P's cocksureness on the mic is well evidenced in tracks like Da 80s, Super Gangsta and Shoot N*****. There are some pretty dope collabos on here too. Ghostface contributes a hype verse on Star Of The State. U Aint Ready 4 Me feat Beanie Sigel is the hardest collabo I've heard all year and that track is also on Beanie Sigel's The Solution(cop it). No Styles P album is complete without Sheek and Jadakiss and they show up on the dope Gangsta Gangsta.  There are some flaws on this album. Let's Go feat Ray J is a complete mismatch and boy does it show. Green Piece Of Paper is ehhh and the Tron-sounding Look At Her is corny as hell. There are tight tracks that have bad guest appearances too. Holiday(feat Max B) is tight but Max B doesnt do the hook justice. In It To Win It is tight also but Bully's appearance was weak. The album does end with a great track, the conscious Cause I'm Black feat Black Thought. Bottom Line: While the album title sounds it was concieved on the spot, this album sounded like it was taken time to make and it pays off. Styles P's wordplay was great, the production was sick and and the guest appearances(with three exceptions) were on point too. This is Styles P's best work to date until he makes another album that tops this one. Standout Tracks: ALONE IN THE STREET, SHOOT N*****, ALL I KNOW IS PAIN, GOT MY EYES ON YOU, DA 80s, SUPER GANGSTER, STAR OF THE STATE, U AINT READY 4 ME and CAUSE IM BLACK.
rows.title: The Phantom is still a menace

rows.text:
Advanced notice: This review will be long and as comprehensive and detailed as I can be with updates as time goes on. If you like short reviews it's 5 stars and skip to the bottom for a summary. If you like details about another perspective and experience read on...

****Update 08-08-2018***
I just got off the phone with Primary Arms tech guy and after asking questions the helpful gentleman looked up the information for me.

To paraphrase the phone call this scope will work with Mk262 from a 16-18" barrel zero'd at 200 yards or 2" high at 100 yards.
It will also work for M80 ball as well as up to 180 grain I forget the bullet and velocity now but iirc it was pretty standard.
Inside the manual for people who don't have an issue reading (bifocals mess with my eyes to much) it sounded like it should work with pretty much every common load for both calibers, but consult the manual yourself by downloading it before purchasing to verify.

So with this information in hand I am incredibly pleased so far and am looking forward to trying to get to the range this evening to revisit some things I mentioned in my original review. I now have my eye relief set as close as I'm able to on my 10/22 and if able to go will be taking a .223Wylde chamber as well!
Yay me!!!!

Also the more I handle this scope it seems like it may well be just about ideal for a DMR optic! Reticle is bold enough to easily see yet not too thick for fairly precise accurate shots. It's not a benchrest scope by any means but a real world user friendly reticle that should work under 98% of all situations needed. Can't wait to try it on deer across a corn field this fall!!!

Yes I'm hopeful and excited about/for this scope and its potential!!!

***End up update****

Original review:

08/02/2018
I received my Primary Arms 4-14X scope today and threw it in a pair of UTG medium rings on top of my semi custom Ruger 1022 just for evaluation purposes. My rifle isn't anything special just a stock Ruger I've had for over 20 years that I finally swapped out the barrel and stock on. Tactical Solutions 16.5" threaded barrel with iron sights and TS muzzle brake, and a Hogue over moulded stock. Nothing too fancy just simple and accurate.

I wasn't able to get to the range until about 7:30-8:00PM so my evaluation was not conducted during optimal light hours. This is relevant especially to a person like myself who has failing eyesight due to a genetic disease that affects my retinas. Translation besides losing my eyesight I can't see at night and have an extremely difficult time in the fading light where most of you see just fine. I almost would be better off to close my eyes or get out a flashlight like it was pitch black outside. I mention and disclose this not for pity or anything other than to illustrate my unique perspective in an attempt to make it easier for you dear reader to get a feel for where my reviews come from. For what it's worth I just can't see at night and do just fine during the day so I'm not "blind" or anything yet but we'll see what I am in another 20 years.

My evaluation was conducted at 50 yards using Remington Thunderbolts and Federal bulk. No Target ammunition because I'm in the process of teaching my kids to shoot and they're not ready for the good stuff yet. They need a little more improvement with the old Remington 514 single shot first before they graduate to scopes etc. Besides that any rifle I've ever had that shot well always did so with Thunderbolts as well as match at 50 & 100 yards.

Targets used were unconventional for accuracy testing however I find them quite suitable for this as well as the fun factor. Clay pigeons and after breaking them on the berm clean up the little pieces with the rifle. Fun for myself and the kids with increasingly smaller targets to shoot with instant visual gratification. So while no measurable accuracy or box tests a fair and fun test of practical accuracy before sandbags and paper.

If you're familiar with my reviews you'll know that I own a variety of optics from Schmidt & Bender to Unertl to Kahles, Hensoldt, Zeiss, Denver Colorado Redfield, Leupold & new Redfield, older Bushnell XXXX Elites, blah blah blah. Enough quality both affordable and not so affordable for most people. I mention this not for penis envy simply like my eyesight to be able to qualify and quantify my opinions. If I feel it's as good or better than X brand Y model then it probably is because I can compare the two should I choose to.

Initial impressions were positive. This is not my first Primary Arms scope so I had a passing familiarity in my expectations. As others have said it's a tank! Well maybe not a tank but it's no lightweight cheap feeling scope. It feels stout and durable.

Turrets moved nicely with crisp clicks I can feel as well as hear. No mush what so ever!

Magnification ring is a little stiff for my likes but it's not unreasonable or unusable. I feel I'll be able to change magnification with winter gloves on just fine. I think firm yet smooth would be an accurate statement regarding it's movement.

I'm drawing a brain fart on the proper terminology however adjusting the ocular end to clean up the blur for my eyesight performed well. I feel that it could've been a little finer however it definitely was not coarse and too abrupt for me. It adjusted quickly and I was able to easily fine tune it for my peepers without feeling like it took all day. So maybe it's just about ideal after all? I'll have to revisit this on my next range trip.

Reticle is perfectly executed, clean, clear etching with no burrs or fuzz. Quite usable on 4X for my failing eyesight although ranging might take me a little more work on 4X than on 10X but while small on 4 it's certainly usable. I'm quite pleased with the reticle and have zero complaints!!!

Illumination is going to be another that I have to revisit simply because I didn't use it at the range. I only checked it out at night after getting home and I can say that 6 appears quite bright for my eyes and 1 while visible at night is likely to be nonexistent during the day. I generally don't use illumination during the day and only what's absolutely needed as light fades. So for now I'm pleased with the illumination.

Slop, wobble, tightness of tolerances or lack there of. Everything I turned, shook, wiggled, etc felt well executed and machined. There is zero wiggle to anything. Threads feel clean and clear, no burrs felt, everything fit tight and really just feels like QUALITY! Movement of things that are supposed to move was smooth as a well groomed woman and like a well groomed woman I have no complaints with either!!!

Eye relief.... Oh the bane of entry level optics!!! This I am going to have to revisit as I need to reposition my rings to get better placement of the scope on the rail. However I felt that from 4-10 eye relief was good. It wasn't super particular however at 11-14 it got a little bitchy and again the scope was not properly positioned so this needs to be revisited. Due to not getting to the range early enough I just threw it all together and did not take the time I should have during set up. So no issue with the scope yet.
I will say that due to the scopes design there is not a lot of room for sliding it around in the rings. Something to be aware of if you have a short rail like a 1022 for example. Likely a non issue in a proper one piece mount versus using super wide rings with six screws each!

Glass quality and clarity... Here's where most entry level optics fail miserably as well and I will revisit this topic as time goes on and I have more experience with this scope. Initially however I want to liken it's clarity to my Zeiss conquest. I'm pretty f-ing pleased there!!! It may be better, it may be worse, but I think that's a good fair starting point for comparison.
My last shots were fired at ~8:30pm and I had no real problems finding the clays or their pieces even though I about went ass over tea kettle on a bench on my way back to the truck. My kid just laughed after making sure I was ok. You can laugh cry or get pissed off in life and I'd rather laugh in a good way because the rest just isn't constructive or helpful so no my kid isn't a dick that's just how we deal with my failing sight.

The boy loved the scope and said to him it was clear and bright up to the end. When asked if he could see better thru it than with the naked eye his reply was basically "Oh yes!!" So the scope does gather light very well and doesn't fail in that fading light most other lower priced optics do.

Of interest to note on 4X clays are boringly easy to break. The horseshoe brackets the clay almost perfectly so it's idiot proof. If you missed a whole clay you really screwed up!!
Also I have to note that I was able to easily clear up the scope to where I could see the ridges on the clay at 50 on 4X.

One thing I have to mention is that I practically ran out of elevation adjustment. IIRC correctly I'm 4 clicks from bottoming out. It occurs to me that I don't recall if the rail I put on the rifle was a 20MOA rail or not and if it was did I put it on backwards? I will revisit this and if I screwed up I will disclose that so you can laugh and shake your head at me to.

I will update this review as time goes on and as I gain more time with this scope. While I'm no bodies fan boy I can't say anything bad really about this scope yet. So in a nutshell...

Sub $300 optic slightly over $300 sitting in new rings.
Extremely clear and bright glass.
Gathers light well. Better than I expected.
Everything that's supposed to move does so smoothly and feels like quality workmanship.
Awesome reticle.
If it continues to perform then it's a solid bargain!!!
Oh my scope did not come with flip up caps but rather one of those rubber bikinis. No points lost here as cheap flip ups suck and bikinis rot so I would've replaced them anyway.

I purchased my scope for full asking price on Amazon receiving no discounts, compensation, or sexual favors for my review. I review everything honestly and try to give the good, bad, ugly, and indifferent in hopes of helping others make educated decisions and to help others out. It is my sincerest hope that this does help people out.
Should you find it helpful please click yes and let Amazon know, however if you find it to be craptastic then by all means click that button as well!

Thanks for reading and again I hope I've helped.
rows.title: Initial impressions Awesome scope at an entry level price!!!
